extra.message: User-controlled input from the 'username' query parameter is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string and rendered via Environment().from_string(...).render(). This constitutes Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), enabling attackers to execute arbitrary Python code on the server (e.g., via payloads like {{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }}). This leads to remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The 'username' query parameter is reflected back to the user inside an HTMLResponse without any escaping/sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (e.g., ?username=<script>alert(1)</script>), leading to reflected Cross-Site Scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
